slides.title: Automated certificate lifecycle management
body: HashiCorp Vault PKI + Ansible Automation Platform
slides.notes: Welcome slide. This presentation covers the end-to-end certificate lifecycle management solution built on HashiCorp Vault and Ansible.

text_boxes.text: Why manual certificate management fails at scale
Seven stages from issuance to rollback
Zero trust authentication and least-privilege access

slides.notes: Agenda overview. Five sections covering the full story from problem to measurable business outcomes.

text_boxes.text: Why manual certificate management fails at enterprise scale

slides.notes: Section 1: The Challenge. Set the stage for why automation is necessary.

text_boxes.text: Manual operations vs. automated lifecycle management

cards.title: Before: Manual operations
body: SSH into servers to manually replace certs
Inconsistent formats across Nginx and Tomcat
Outages from expired certificates at 2 AM

cards.title: After: Automated with Vault + Ansible
body: Vault PKI auto-issues certificates on demand
Ansible deploys to any service type in minutes

slides.notes: The contrast between manual and automated operations. Every pain point on the left maps to a solution on the right.

text_boxes.text: Average cost per certificate-related outage: $300,000+ in lost revenue and remediation

slides.notes: Let the number land. 73% is a staggering figure. This is the cost of inaction.

text_boxes.text: Enterprise certificate distribution by service type

cards.title: Web servers -- Nginx (40%)
cards.body: PEM format: cert.pem + key.pem + chain.pem, graceful reload

cards.title: Application servers -- Tomcat (30%)
cards.body: PKCS12 keystore format, requires JVM restart for reload

rich_text.text: Key challenge:
Each service type requires different certificate formats, deployment methods, and reload strategies.

slides.notes: The diversity of certificate formats and deployment methods is the core complexity. Our dispatcher pattern solves this.

text_boxes.text: Vault PKI + Ansible automation platform architecture

slides.notes: Section 2: The Solution. Introduce the three pillars of the architecture.

text_boxes.text: Integrated platform for the complete certificate lifecycle

cards.title: HashiCorp Vault
body: PKI secrets engine for X.509 certs

cards.title: Ansible automation
body: 7 modular roles for each lifecycle stage
Serial execution for blast radius control

rich_text.text: IBM advantage:
All three components are IBM products with unified enterprise support, compliance certifications, and professional services.

slides.notes: Each pillar handles a distinct concern: Vault for PKI, Ansible for deployment logic, AAP for enterprise orchestration.
Five-layer architecture from AAP orchestration down to target hosts. Each layer is independently testable and replaceable.

text_boxes.text: Integrated toolchain for certificate lifecycle management

slides.notes: Each component maps to a specific technology. The Ansible collections (community.hashi_vault and community.crypto) are the glue.

text_boxes.text: Seven stages from issuance to emergency rollback

slides.notes: Section 3: Certificate Lifecycle. Walk through each stage in detail.

text_boxes.text: Four-stage pipeline with automatic rollback on failure

cards.title: Issue
body: Returns cert, key, and CA chain

cards.title: Deploy
body: Write cert, key, and chain files

slides.notes: The block/rescue pattern in Ansible ensures that if any stage fails, the entire pipeline rolls back automatically.

text_boxes.text: One deploy role, multiple service handlers

cards.title: Tomcat deployment
body: Retrieves keystore password from Vault KV
Converts PEM to PKCS12 keystore format
Full service restart required by JVM

rich_text.text: Extensibility:
Add new service types by creating deploy_<type>.yml -- no changes to existing code required.

slides.notes: The dispatcher pattern is the key architectural decision. It makes the system extensible without modifying core logic.

text_boxes.text: Proactive certificate management with daily health checks

rich_text.text: How it works:
clm_renew.yml runs daily via AAP schedule. Checks expiry on all hosts. If < 30 days remaining, triggers full issue-deploy-verify pipeline.

slides.notes: The sparklines show certificate days-to-expiry trending down, automatic renewal events, and zero unexpected expirations.

cards.title: Certificate revocation
body: Revoke by serial number in Vault

cards.title: Emergency rollback
body: Manual trigger via AAP or CLI

cards.title: Block/rescue pattern
body: Automatic rollback if deploy or verify fails
Provides full failure details in output

cards.title: Design decision
body: Revocation is never automatic on rollback
Separates failure recovery from deliberate action

slides.notes: Key design decision: revocation and rollback are intentionally separate. Rollback is fast recovery; revocation is a deliberate security action.

text_boxes.text: Zero trust authentication and least-privilege access controls

slides.notes: Section 4: Security and Compliance. Critical for enterprise adoption.

text_boxes.text: Three layers of defense for certificate operations

cards.title: Authorization
body: Issue and revoke only -- no admin
PKI mount scoped to one role

slides.notes: Three pillars of security: how we authenticate, what we authorize, and how we audit. No shortcuts.

slides.title: Vault policy -- least-privilege access

rich_text.text: Principle:
AAP can only issue, revoke, and read certificates -- it has no access to Vault admin, sys, or auth paths.

slides.notes: Walk through each policy path. Emphasize that the policy file (aap_pki_policy.hcl) is version-controlled and auditable.

text_boxes.text: Four safety mechanisms that prevent cascading failures
Serial execution (one host at a time)
Playbooks use serial: 1 to process hosts sequentially. If one host fails, remaining hosts are untouched.
If deployment or verification fails, the rescue block restores the previous certificate and re-verifies.
File permission checks plus live TLS handshake validation against the running service before marking success.
Last 5 certificate backups retained on each host. Emergency rollback restores from the most recent backup.

slides.notes: Each safety mechanism is independent. Together, they ensure that a certificate deployment failure never causes a service outage.

text_boxes.text: Enterprise orchestration with Ansible Automation Platform

slides.notes: Section 5: AAP Integration. How this moves from CLI automation to enterprise-grade operations.

text_boxes.text: Each lifecycle operation maps to a dedicated AAP job template

slides.notes: Five playbooks, five job templates. The renewal job runs on schedule; everything else is manual or event-driven.

text_boxes.text: Vault AppRole credential type injects Role ID and Secret ID as environment variables
Per-run overrides for target hosts, CN, SANs, TTL, and service type
Daily 2 AM renewal check runs automatically via AAP schedule
Slack, email, and webhook alerts on job failure or certificate issues
Role-based access controls separate operators from approvers and auditors
Every job run is logged with inputs, outputs, timestamps, and user identity

slides.notes: AAP adds the enterprise wrapper: credentials, scheduling, notifications, RBAC, and audit. This is what separates a script from a platform.
Section 6: Business Impact. Quantify the value.
Four dimensions of certificate automation maturity. All four areas are at or above 85% automation.

rich_text.text: Bottom line:
Certificate operations that took hours now take minutes, with zero manual intervention for renewals.

slides.notes: Let the numbers speak for themselves. Each metric maps to a specific pain point from the challenge section.

text_boxes.text: Vault PKI engine setup, intermediate CA, AppRole configuration
Ansible roles development, dispatcher pattern, testing
AAP job templates, surveys, credentials, scheduling
Rollout to all hosts, monitoring, daily renewal schedule enabled

rich_text.text: Timeline:
8 weeks from kickoff to fully automated certificate lifecycle management in production.

slides.notes: Walk through each phase. Emphasize that the modular design allows parallel work across phases.

rich_text.text: 1. Vault PKI eliminates manual certificate management.
Dynamic X.509 certificate issuance with built-in CA hierarchy, CRL management, and revocation tracking.
2. Ansible automation handles every service type.
The dispatcher pattern deploys to Nginx, Tomcat, and future service types without modifying existing code.
3. Safety is built into every operation.
Serial execution, block/rescue rollback, two-stage verification, and timestamped backups prevent cascading failures.
4. AAP makes it enterprise-grade.
Scheduling, RBAC, notifications, audit trails, and survey-driven workflows turn automation into a platform.

slides.notes: Summary slide. Each callout reinforces a key message. Use this as the final content slide before the closing.

text_boxes.text: Every certificate, on every server, managed automatically -- from issuance to renewal to revocation.
HashiCorp Vault + Ansible Automation Platform makes this possible today.

slides.notes: Let the statement land. Pause for 3-5 seconds. This is the final message before the IBM logo.
Thank you slide. IBM logo. Open for questions.
